message:
fix(service): track ready-slot reservations in Buffer to prevent capacity leaks

Buffer::poll_ready() increments the pending count to reserve a capacity slot
but if the caller never follows up with call() (e.g., the task is cancelled
or the Buffer clone is dropped), the slot is permanently leaked, eventually
exhausting capacity.

Fix by adding a ready_slot_reserved bool to each Buffer handle:
- poll_ready() sets it when a slot is claimed
- call() clears it when the slot is consumed
- Drop releases the slot if still reserved
- Clone does NOT inherit the reservation (fresh handle, no claim)

Extract release_capacity_claim() as a shared helper used by PendingGuard::drop,
BufferFuture::drop, and the new Buffer::drop, eliminating three copies of
identical waker-notification logic.

poll_ready() now returns Ok(()) immediately if a slot is already reserved,
avoiding double-counting when poll_ready() is called multiple times before
call(). The pending count is incremented under the lock and the ready_slot_reserved
flag is set atomically afterward.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
